mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances following a >0.2pp UNRATE increase.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized strength in consumer discretionary and residential investment demand, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive changes in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a win rate above 50% over out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during periods of accommodative monetary policy.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases yield negative 5-day returns during easing cycles, or the signal loses predictive power when FEDFUNDS is below 1.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month CPI increase exceeding 0.4% triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening and margin compression, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in > 60% of months where CPIAUCSL rises above 0.4%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a decline in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE produces a negative 60-day return win rate below 45% across historical expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly increases in real gross domestic product reflect broad-based economic growth and corporate earnings expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth shows no predictive relationship with 20-day forward returns, with Rank IC consistently below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5% signals a sharp contraction in manufacturing activity and impending earnings downgrades, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 yields positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances following an INDPRO drop below -0.5%.
